blocking_finding.fact_1: Every corpus segment (CMU 304, KungfuAthleteBot 791, Kyokushin 1442 = 2537 total) is a SINGLE-ACTOR window: keys are posed_joints[120,34,3] + root_positions[120,3]. No opponent surface exists anywhere in the training/eval data.
blocking_finding.fact_2: contact_frame is a window marker (only 30/304 CMU segments even use frames-1); it does not mark a two-body contact event.
blocking_finding.fact_3: The declared hard gate 'held-out hand-to-opponent-surface error <=15mm' therefore CANNOT be evaluated on this corpus. The trainer's |hand_z - 0.65m| metric is an absolute-reach proxy, not the gate.
blocking_finding.fact_4: Prior claim 'no licensed teacher reaches 650mm (max ~0.44m)' was WRONG. Real CMU grab reach at contact: median 392.8mm, mean 463.8mm, p95 1125.4mm; 31/255 reach >=650mm.
blocking_finding.fact_5: Two sound proxy attempts recorded: v6 concat median 365.10mm FAIL; v8 clean corpus median 27.10mm FAIL (5/48 <=15mm, ablation +222.94mm). These are proxy numbers, not gate numbers.

exact_unblock: Build paired two-actor segments: attacker posed_joints + defender (opponent) surface in ONE shared coordinate frame, from a dual-actor licensed source (Harmony4D grappling pairs or CMU paired clips), with per-frame hand-to-opponent-surface distance measurable. Then re-run the identical evaluator measuring hand-to-opponent-surface distance over the secure-grab window at GRAB_ACQUIRE_RANGE_MM=650. No gate weakening: 650mm acquisition semantics and the 15mm surface threshold are preserved.

gates.hand_to_opponent_surface_median_mm: BLOCKED (not evaluable: no opponent surface in corpus)

not_permitted: relabel single-actor clips as two-actor grabs
weaken the 15mm surface threshold or the 650mm acquisition constant
